threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'grocery store' instead of a 'market'—only locals know the real gems hidden behind warehouse doors.
Caught a guy on the 47 last night, loudly reciting poetry to no one, while a couple argued about their takeaway choices. Classic South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy selling homemade pickles out of a backpack on Hermannstraße, I knew I’d found my place in the chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 4 AM and then hear a spontaneous techno party erupt in the street. It’s a mix of culinary delight and pure chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they skip the long brunch lines and just grab a burrito from El Farolito at 10 AM, no hesitation.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing says San Francisco like that one Muni driver who stops to help a lost tourist with directions, while the bus waits, and the delay doesn't even faze them.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all the local bars serve tapas, but many still do pintxos on toothpicks, and you'll miss it if you don't explore beyond the main square.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they still stare at the metro maps, trying to decipher the L2 lines like it’s a puzzle, while locals breeze past.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'London Fields' instead of 'The Fields' and look shocked when someone tells you to get the bus to the Lido.
Caught a night bus and overheard a guy telling his mate he once mistook a kebab shop for a dating app. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the same guy feeding the ducks at the canal every Sunday, I realized this place has its own rhythm. It's small moments like that.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those spontaneous street parties in Kreuzberg? One moment it's just a quiet road, then bam—a whole block is dancing under the stars, no planning, just vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they dodge the brunch crowds and hit the taco trucks at midnight instead.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that turned into a pop-up dinner spot—two guys grilling next to the stop. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just a picturesque spot, but they miss the vibrant street art and the local bars that come alive after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map, staring at it like it’s a puzzle. Locals just pull out their phones and get on with it.
